title: Netskope Cloud App Risk Assessment
spl:
index=casb sourcetype="netskope:events" earliest=-7d
| where cci_score < 50
| stats dc(user) as users, sum(numbytes) as bytes by app_name, cci_score, category
| eval gb=round(bytes/1073741824,2)
| sort -users
description: Netskope assigns Cloud Confidence Index (CCI) scores to SaaS applications. Tracking high-risk (low CCI) app usage reveals shadow IT and data exposure. Trending CCI across the organization supports SaaS governance and vendor risk management.
value: Netskope assigns Cloud Confidence Index (CCI) scores to SaaS applications. Tracking high-risk (low CCI) app usage reveals shadow IT and data exposure. Trending CCI across the organization supports SaaS governance and vendor risk management.
implementation: Configure the Netskope Add-on REST input for application events. Map `cci_score` (0‚Äď100) to risk tiers (0‚Äď30 critical, 31‚Äď50 high). Alert when new high-risk apps appear with >5 users. Report weekly on shadow IT growth and data volume to unsanctioned apps.
visualization: Table (risky apps by users and data volume), Pie chart (CCI distribution), Bar chart (top unsanctioned apps), Line chart (shadow IT trend).
knownFalsePositives: Spikes from new SaaS rollouts, automated workflows, sanctioned bulk uploads, or M&A integration.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Netskope Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3808).
‚ÄĘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=netskope:events` or `netskope:application`.
‚ÄĘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
Configure the Netskope Add-on REST input for application events. Map `cci_score` (0‚Äď100) to risk tiers (0‚Äď30 critical, 31‚Äď50 high). Alert when new high-risk apps appear with >5 users. Report weekly on shadow IT growth and data volume to unsanctioned apps.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=casb sourcetype="netskope:events" earliest=-7d
| where cci_score < 50
| stats dc(user) as users, sum(numbytes) as bytes by app_name, cci_score, category
| eval gb=round(bytes/1073741824,2)
| sort -users
```

Understanding this SPL

**Netskope Cloud App Risk Assessment** ‚ÄĒ Netskope assigns Cloud Confidence Index (CCI) scores to SaaS applications. Tracking high-risk (low CCI) app usage reveals shadow IT and data exposure. Trending CCI across the organization supports SaaS governance and vendor risk management.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=netskope:events` or `netskope:application`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Netskope Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3808). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚ÄĒrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: casb; **sourcetype**: netskope:events. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚ÄĘ Scopes the data: index=casb, sourcetype="netskope:events", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚ÄĘ Filters the current rows with `where cci_score < 50` ‚ÄĒ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚ÄĘ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_name, cci_score, category** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚ÄĘ `eval` defines or adjusts **gb** ‚ÄĒ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚ÄĘ Orders rows with `sort` ‚ÄĒ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Compare with Netskope CASB and tenant admin for the same users, apps, and time window.


Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (risky apps by users and data volume), Pie chart (CCI distribution), Bar chart (top unsanctioned apps), Line chart (shadow IT trend).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
hardware: Netskope Security Cloud (cloud-delivered), Netskope Client (endpoint agent)
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
